description_long: This is a compact, hexagonal pagoda structure featuring two distinct, stacked roof tiers with prominent upward-curving eaves. The roofs are covered in blue-green ceramic tiles, each tier separated by a band of ornate stonework and dark wooden walls with decorative panels. The base is solid and hexagonal, constructed from stone, providing a sturdy foundation. At the apex of the upper roof sits a pointed ornamental finial, adding a traditional flourish. The overall design is reminiscent of classical Chinese pagodas, with intricate details and a harmonious blend of stone, wood, and tile. The structure is visually striking and would serve as a decorative or spiritual landmark in an urban park or garden.
description: A small, hexagonal Chinese-style pagoda with two blue-green tiled roof tiers, ornate stonework, and a pointed finial. The structure features upward-curving eaves and detailed wooden panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical hexagonal shape, two stacked tiled roofs, and the central ornamental finial.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the layered roof tiles, upward-curving eaves, and the detailed stone base.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with clear visibility of the roof tiers and decorative finial.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, emphasizing the hexagonal base, roof curvature, and wooden wall panels.
